title: audit-barrage-config.yaml — multi-model audit-barrage CLI battery
description: Machine-readable spec of the audit-barrage model config consumed by `dw-lifecycle audit-barrage`. Project-overridable at `.dw-lifecycle/scope-discovery/audit-barrage-config.yaml`; the plugin's shipped default at `plugins/dw-lifecycle/templates/audit-barrage-config.yaml` is the fallback. Each entry registers one CLI tool that fires against the rendered audit prompt in parallel; per-model output is captured under `.dw-lifecycle/scope-discovery/audit-runs/<timestamp>-<feature>/`. The runtime parser (plugins/dw-lifecycle/src/scope-discovery/audit-barrage/config-loader.ts) is the authoritative validator; this schema mirrors its contract for editor autocomplete + inline validation.

models.description: The registered model battery. Order is preserved — the orchestrator fires entries in this order (parallel fan-out; order only affects INDEX.md row sequencing). At least one entry required. Entry names must be unique.

ModelEntry.description: One CLI to fire as part of the audit barrage. Per the audit-barrage-cli-notes.md probe, every CLI must (a) be installed + on PATH or referenced by absolute path; (b) accept the prompt as a single argv element; (c) close stdin cleanly without operator interaction.

name.description: Operator-visible identifier for the model. Used as the per-model filename stem in the run dir (lightly sanitized for filesystem safety) and to filter via the `--models` flag. Must be non-empty. Convention: lowercase kebab-case matches the CLI's own name (`claude`, `codex`, `gemini`).

binary.description: Path or PATH-resolvable name of the CLI binary to spawn. Resolved by `child_process.spawn` against the operator's PATH. Use an absolute path when the binary lives outside PATH or when multiple installs need disambiguation. Must be non-empty.

args_template.description: Whitespace-delimited argv template. MUST contain the literal `{{prompt}}` placeholder, which the spawn helper substitutes with the rendered audit prompt as a single argv element (the substitution happens after the template is split on whitespace, so the prompt itself can contain whitespace without being mangled). Examples from the v1 default battery: `"-p {{prompt}}"` (claude), `"exec {{prompt}}"` (codex), `"{{prompt}}"` (gemini).

timeout_seconds.description: Wall-clock budget in seconds. On expiry, the subprocess is sent SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after a 5-second grace. Must be a positive integer. The v1 default is 300 (5 minutes), which gives 20-60x headroom over measured round-trip times for typical audit prompts.
